title: Savory Moroccan Yukon Gold Potatoes Stir-fry
description: A sour and juicy Moroccan dish featuring slow-cooked yukon gold potatoes with a blend of aromatic spices for an adventurous low-calorie-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chopped for sweetness and depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced finely, adds savory umami notes

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Ground spice for earthy flavor profile

ingredients.name: Coriander (Cilantro)
ingredients.notes: Chopped fresh, garnish and adds freshness to the dish

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Rinse Yukon Gold Potatoes and slice into 1/2-inch thick rounds.
Place potato slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, drizzle with olive oil evenly, sprinkle cumin over potatoes and toss until coated.
Bake for about 35 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from oven.
While potatoes are baking, prepare the seasoning mix: In a small bowl, combine tomato paste, garlic, salt to taste, and fresh coriander leaves; mix well.
Heat olive oil in a wok over medium heat. Add onion slices and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add prepared seasoning mix, stir-fry for another minute until fragrant. Push the onions to one side of the wok.
Once potatoes are done, remove them from oven and add back into the wok with the onions. Stir thoroughly to coat.
Serve hot over a bed of steamed green vegetables or as a side dish alongside grilled meats.
